prompts.text: 'Primary colours' — red, yellow, blue — cannot be made by mixing. 'Secondary colours' — orange, green, purple — each made from two primaries. Use both terms naturally throughout.
Before every mix, ask: 'I wonder what [A] and [B] will make?' Never tell them first. The discovery is the learning — give them time to predict.
Model the cause-and-effect sentence: '[A] and [B] make [result].' Say it together after each mix. Repeat the pattern every time — this is the language of cause and effect.
Point to a colour on the wheel: 'Is this one closer to red or yellow? Why?' — builds understanding that colours exist on a spectrum, not just in fixed categories.
